heading: Limitations
body:
- An organization with an existing subscription that includes support can only join
  a paying organization with the same level of support.

- An organization without a subscription can join a paying organization with
  a [subscription](subscription-types) of any type.

- You can link a paying organization to a maximum of 250 other organizations,
  either manually through the Atlas UI or [programatically](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-createorganization) via the Atlas Admin API.
  To link more than 250 organizations, [MongoDB Support](request-support).

- A paying organization can't already be a linked organization.

- Paying and linked organizations in a cross-org billing configuration:

  - Can't activate self-serve support plans.

  - Can't configure a self-serve marketplace subscription.

  - Share a single support plan (if active).

> **Note:** To purchase a subscription that enables cross-organization billing,
> contact [MongoDB Sales](https://www.mongodb.com/company/contact?tck=docs).

To configure a paying organization and link other organizations to it:

If you wish to create a new organization through which to pay,
[Create an Atlas organization](create-organization).

> **Note:** You can [rename an organization](rename-organization) to
> reach your desired cross-organization configuration.

If you are configuring a new paying organization, click
Start Linking.

If your organization is already a paying organization, click
Link More Organizations.

A dialog box displays your selected organization under
Paying organization on the left and a list of
organizations you may link to it under
Select organizations to link on the right.

Select each organization you wish to link to your
paying organization.

Click Review and Finish.

Confirm that your organizations are linked as intended and click
Finish.

The **Linked Organizations** tab displays your linked
organizations. To link additional organizations, click
**Link More Organizations**.
